diagnosis: The Fisher Transform strategy converts the midpoint of a rolling high-low range into a Gaussian-distributed oscillator using Ehlers' formula. The computation is done inline per bar on a 150-bar trailing slice (not a precomputed column), with a 0.5-weighted smoothing pass before the log transform and another 0.5-weighted recursive blend of the Fisher value itself. Two signal branches exist: (1) bearish reversal when Fisher crosses downward from above +threshold, producing side="short"; (2) bullish reversal when Fisher crosses upward from below -threshold, producing side="long". The short branch is fully live — the strategy emits short signals with an inverted stop-loss (close + ATR_mult) and inverted take-profit (close - ATR_mult), and the backtester executes them as short positions. This means roughly half of all signals are short-side and the strategy is currently bi-directional. The ADX filter caps at adx_max=35 (adx_filter=True), blocking entries during extreme-trend regimes. An optional divergence filter exists (currently disabled). The evolved PARAMS (gen 190, 1104 evals) report WF_Sharpe=2.19 and only 34 trades, indicating extreme sparsity — approximately one trade per month over the training window, making Sharpe estimates highly noise-driven at this sample size. The core Fisher edge is mean-reversion at extremes: the oscillator is expected to revert sharply when price reaches an unusually high or low position within its recent range. In a spot long-only context the short branch must be dropped entirely. The residual long branch fires only on confirmed downward exhaustion (Fisher < -threshold crossing up), which is a legitimate oversold-bounce entry concept. Weakness: with only the long branch active, trade frequency will roughly halve, exacerbating the sparse-signal problem. The recursive Fisher blend (each bar's Fisher = 0.5*new + 0.5*prev) introduces substantial lag, meaning crossings occur well after the actual turning point. The ADX cap further restricts entries to non-trending environments, which is appropriate for mean-reversion but limits the strategy's participation in crypto's dominant trending regimes.
long_only_hypothesis: In spot crypto markets, after a sustained decline that pushes the Fisher Transform below a deeply negative threshold (e.g., -1.8 to -2.2), the exhaustion of selling pressure combined with a Fisher crossover of its signal line marks a probabilistic turning point. The long entry at that crossover, confirmed by the asset being in a non-extreme-trend environment (ADX cap), captures the mean-reversion bounce from oversold range extremes. The position is held with a hard ATR-based stop below entry and an ATR-based take-profit, with a 24-hour time stop as a fallback. The hypothesis is that Fisher values below -threshold identify periods where price has spent an unusual proportion of recent bars near the range low, and the crossover signals that this pressure is abating — consistent with a bounce. In cash during all other periods (no short, no position). Edge is regime-conditional: works in consolidation/mean-reversion regimes, fails in persistent downtrends where Fisher stays pinned negative for extended periods.
market_techniques: Mean-reversion entry at oscillator extremes (Fisher < -threshold crossover)
ATR-normalized stop-loss and take-profit for volatility-adaptive risk management
Regime gating via ADX cap to avoid entering mean-reversion trades in strong trends
Optional bullish divergence filter (price lower low + Fisher higher low) to reduce false positives
Time stop as secondary exit mechanism to cap exposure when price stalls after entry
Strength-weighted position sizing based on Fisher magnitude and crossover gap
Walk-forward validation with embargo gaps to calibrate threshold and ATR multipliers on pre-lockbox data only
useful_features: momentum: rsi_7 — secondary oversold confirmation; require RSI < 40 at Fisher long signal to avoid catching falling knives
momentum: rsi_14 — medium-term momentum alignment; RSI diverging upward while Fisher crosses supports entry
trend: adx_14 — existing gate; keep as filter but consider also gating on ADX direction (falling ADX signals trend exhaustion)
volatility: atr_14 — already used for stop and take-profit sizing; normalize Fisher threshold by recent ATR regime
volatility: bb_pct_b — Bollinger %B below 0.2 at Fisher signal strengthens the oversold-bounce hypothesis
volatility: gk_vol — Garman-Klass vol regime; avoid entries during extreme vol spikes (gap risk)
volume: volume_ratio — above-average volume on the crossover bar confirms buyer absorption at the low
volume: obv_divergence — positive OBV divergence (OBV rising while price falling) strengthens long signal
price/return: dist_from_low_20 — small negative value (price near 20-bar low) confirms the Fisher signal is at a genuine range extreme
price/return: ret_zscore_20 — very negative z-score corroborates oversold conditions
market regime: bmsb_bullish — macro long-only filter; only trade long when price is above BMSB (above bull market support band) to avoid catching falling knives in bear markets
market regime: bb_bw_percentile — low bandwidth percentile signals compression before expansion; avoid Fisher longs when volatility is in extreme compression (breakout risk)
drawdown: dist_from_high_20 — deeply negative values confirm the strategy is entering after a drawdown, consistent with the mean-reversion hypothesis

genome_params.name: period
genome_params.note: Rolling high-low window for Fisher computation; wider = smoother but more lag; 10-18 is sweet spot for hourly crypto

genome_params.name: signal_threshold
genome_params.note: Absolute Fisher value required for a valid signal; higher = rarer but stronger; long-only version should test higher end to keep signal quality high

genome_params.name: adx_max
genome_params.note: Maximum ADX allowed for entry; lower values enforce stronger mean-reversion regime gate

genome_params.name: stop_loss_atr_mult
genome_params.note: ATR multiplier for hard stop below entry; tighter stops cut losses but increase whipsaw exits

genome_params.name: take_profit_atr_mult
genome_params.note: ATR multiplier for take-profit; ratio to stop_loss_atr_mult drives R:R; keep TP/SL >= 1.5

genome_params.name: time_stop_hours
genome_params.note: Maximum hours in trade before forced exit; 24h is reasonable for hourly bars but 48-72h allows more room for mean-reversion to play out

genome_params.name: require_divergence
genome_params.note: Whether to require price-Fisher bullish divergence confirmation; adds selectivity at cost of frequency

genome_params.name: divergence_lookback
genome_params.note: Bars to look back for divergence check; only relevant when require_divergence=True

genome_params.name: rsi_oversold_threshold
genome_params.note: NEW: RSI_14 must be below this value at Fisher long signal; adds momentum confirmation; 35 is a reasonable starting point

genome_params.name: require_bmsb_above
genome_params.note: NEW: Only take Fisher long signals when price is above BMSB (bull-market regime gate); avoids catching falling knives in bear markets

do_not_optimize: allowed_regimes — hard-coding regime labels couples the strategy to the regime classifier's internal state; the ADX filter already provides a cleaner structural gate; evolving allowed_regimes overfits to the specific regime label distribution in the training data
volume_threshold — currently disabled (require_volume=False); adding it to the sweep with only 34 total trades risks fitting to volume noise in a handful of bars

divergence_lookback — extremely high risk of overfitting when require_divergence itself is a binary sweep parameter; if divergence is off, this param is irrelevant, creating a dimension of pure noise in the genome
the exact Fisher smoothing constant (0.5) — Ehlers' original constant; changing it transforms the indicator into something non-standard with unpredictable behavior and no theoretical grounding

risk_management.stop: Hard ATR stop below entry: stop_loss = entry_price - stop_loss_atr_mult * atr_14; default mult 2.0-2.5; the stop level is set at signal time and must not be moved downward once the position is open
risk_management.take_profit: ATR-based take-profit: take_profit = entry_price + take_profit_atr_mult * atr_14; default mult 3.5-4.0; R:R target >= 1.5:1 to compensate for sub-50% hit rate typical of counter-trend entries
risk_management.trailing: No trailing stop recommended for mean-reversion trades; trailing stops tend to give back too much of short-lived bounce gains; rely instead on fixed TP or time stop
risk_management.vol_target: Volatility-scaled sizing using gk_vol or atr_14/close as a proxy for daily vol; target annualized vol fraction of 0.15-0.25 of equity per trade; cap max size at 30% of equity per position to prevent single-trade ruin
risk_management.max_size: Hard cap at 30% of equity per position regardless of vol-target formula output; with only 1 position allowed at a time (position is not None -> return None) this is effectively the maximum exposure
risk_management.cooldown: Minimum 24-hour cooldown between new long entries after a stop-loss exit; prevents rapid re-entry into a continuing downtrend (whipsaw protection); time_stop itself provides natural spacing for time-stop and TP exits
risk_management.risk_off: Regime gate: (1) ADX gate already implemented via adx_max — do not enter if adx_14 > threshold; (2) NEW macro gate: skip all Fisher long signals when price is below both bmsb_sma and bmsb_ema (bear market); (3) Circuit-breaker from RiskManager already halts new entries after sustained equity drawdown; (4) Optional: gate on gk_vol percentile — skip if current gk_vol is in top 10% of trailing 90-day distribution (extreme volatility = gap risk for mean-reversion entries)

metrics: CAGR — primary return metric; target > 20% annualized in spot long-only; normalize by comparing to BTC buy-and-hold CAGR over same OOS window
Sharpe ratio (annualized) — target > 1.0 on 2-year OOS lockbox; WF-reported 2.19 is on a short in-sample window and will almost certainly not hold OOS
Sortino ratio — more relevant than Sharpe for long-only strategies since only downside deviation matters; target > 1.5
Max drawdown % — hard constraint <= 25% on full OOS equity curve; currently evolved PARAMS have only 34 trades so per-fold drawdowns are unreliable
Calmar ratio (CAGR / maxDD) — target > 0.8 for the strategy to be viable; mean-reversion strategies often have poor Calmar in trending markets
Hit rate (% winning trades) — counter-trend mean-reversion typically targets 45-55% hit rate; below 40% with current R:R of ~1.8:1 means negative EV
Number of trades — minimum 30 trades over 2-year OOS period required for any statistical inference; with the short branch removed this is a serious concern
Average trade duration (hours) — expected 12-48h for a mean-reversion bounce; longer durations suggest the time stop is doing heavy lifting
Turnover / exposure % — fraction of time with an open position; low exposure (< 20%) means the strategy is mostly in cash, which is structurally safe but reduces absolute return
Cost sensitivity — with wide stops (2.2 ATR) and few trades, commission/slippage sensitivity should be low; verify by running at 2x and 3x cost assumptions
Bootstrap / Monte Carlo on trade sequence — with only 34 trades, resample with replacement 1000x to get confidence intervals on Sharpe and maxDD; critical for a sparse strategy
Per-year Sharpe stability — compute rolling 365-day Sharpe to detect year-over-year regime dependency; a strategy that worked in 2021 bear and failed in 2023 bull is not robust
sweep_changes: DROP the short branch entirely: remove lines 119-121 (side='short' assignment) and the short-side stop_loss/take_profit calculation (lines 155-157); the strategy should only return a Signal when side='long' or return None

Remove 'short' from allowed_regimes and from any regime-gating logic; the allowed_regimes list is only relevant if require_trend_regime is True, and 'lateral' and 'mean_reversion' are the relevant long-only regimes

ADD rsi_oversold_threshold parameter: at signal bar require rsi_14 < rsi_oversold_threshold (suggested sweep range 25-45) as a secondary oversold confirmation gate; read rsi_14 from current bar (already in features)
ADD bmsb_bullish macro gate: read bmsb_bullish from current bar; if False (price below both BMSB SMA and EMA), return None — this prevents counter-trend longs in sustained bear markets
ADD volume confirmation option: add require_vol_confirm boolean; when True require volume_ratio > 1.0 at the crossover bar (buyer absorption), controlled by a separate bool sweep param
EXTEND time_stop_hours sweep range to (12, 72) from current (24, 24) fixed value — time stop is a critical exit for this sparse mean-reversion strategy and should be part of the genome
ADD dist_from_low_20 gate: require dist_from_low_20 > -0.15 (price within 15% of 20-bar low) to ensure Fisher signal coincides with a genuine range-bottom approach, not a mid-range false trigger
EXTEND period sweep upper bound to 24 (from 20) to allow testing of slower, more stable Fisher configurations appropriate for hourly crypto data

SCALE signal strength calculation to use only abs(ft) relative to threshold, not absolute: strength = min(1.0, 0.5 + (abs(ft) - threshold) / threshold) — this makes the sizing formula regime-agnostic rather than hardcoded to the Fisher scale

ADD obv_divergence as optional filter: if obv_divergence > 0 at signal bar (OBV above its 20-SMA while price is near lows), add 0.1 to strength; implement as a read-only informative adjustment, not a gate, to preserve trade frequency

ENSURE the strategy handles the long-only conversion cleanly: return None for any bar where the short condition would have fired, and confirm in unit tests that no Signal with side='short' is ever emitted after the change
methodological_risks: Extreme sparsity (34 trades in-sample, ~17 long-only after short removal): standard Sharpe and Calmar estimates have huge standard errors with N < 30; WF_Sharpe=2.19 could easily be 0.5 with a different random seed or slightly different window; bootstrap confidence intervals are mandatory before any promotion decision

Recursive Fisher smoothing creates lag that is period-dependent AND value-history-dependent; when the backtester slices only 150 bars per call and recomputes from scratch each time, the Fisher state at bar 0 of each slice initializes to 0.0 rather than the true running value — this introduces a systematic warm-up bias where early Fisher values in each slice are pulled toward zero, potentially generating spurious crossovers at slice boundaries

The evolved params (gen 190) were optimized including the short branch; signal_threshold=1.982 may have been tuned to balance both long and short signal frequencies; removing the short branch changes the effective signal distribution and the evolved threshold may no longer be optimal for long-only

ADX cap at 35.3 was co-evolved with the short branch; without shorts, the optimal ADX regime may differ — the current cap may be too permissive or too restrictive for long-only mean-reversion entries specifically

Time stop at 24 hours is currently fixed (not in the sweep); for hourly bars this means the position can only be held for 24 candles, which is extremely short for a mean-reversion trade that may take 2-5 days to play out; many valid bounces will be time-stopped before reaching TP, degrading the R:R assumption

Overfitting risk in the 2-year OOS lockbox: with only ~17 expected long-only trades in 2 years (roughly 1 per 6 weeks), the OOS evaluation has near-zero statistical power — a single large winning trade can make the strategy look excellent while it is structurally unprofitable

The 150-bar lookback slice cap means that for large bar_idx the Fisher is computed on a fixed 150-bar window regardless of the period parameter; for period=24 this is only 6.25x the period, which may be insufficient for the recursive smoothing to stabilize

Regime label dependency: the allowed_regimes gate includes 'trend' (currently enabled), which is inconsistent with the ADX cap designed to exclude trending environments; these two filters may cancel each other or create confusing regime combinations that overfit to the specific regime classifier's output distribution
